title: Wire Google Custom Search engine ID into backend search integration

requirements.summary: Verify frontend search flow correctly passes queries to backend, receives Google Custom Search results, and displays them without errors
acceptanceCriteria: Typing a search query in the address bar and pressing Enter triggers a search request to the backend.
Search results are rendered in the SearchResults component with title, snippet, and display URL.
Clicking a search result navigates the active tab to the result URL via the proxy.
No console errors appear during a normal search flow.

file_operations.description: Review and verify the useSearchRequest mutation correctly calls the backend's proxyGoogleSearch method and handles the response. Ensure error handling is in place for failed search requests.

Review the search flow integration to ensure search queries from the address bar are passed to useSearchRequest mutation, results are stored in the active tab state, and error states are properly handled and displayed to the user.

Verify the component correctly parses and renders the search results returned from the backend, including title, snippet, and displayLink fields. Ensure the onNavigate callback is properly wired for result clicks.
Review the search detection logic to ensure queries (non-URL input) trigger the search flow rather than proxy navigation. Verify the Enter key and submit button correctly invoke the search handler.
